reasoning: The question asks if there will be more fatalities in North Macedonia in the 30 days before 2026-03-08 (Feb 6 to Mar 7) than the 30-day average over the prior year. The reference 30-day average is 0.083 (representing exactly 1 fatality over the 360-day period). Since any number of fatalities in the target 30-day period must be an integer, 'more than the average' translates directly to 'at least 1 ACLED-recorded fatality' in this exact 30-day window. North Macedonia has very low rates of political violence (averaging ~1 ACLED fatality per year). For a generic 30-day window, the baseline probability is around 8%. We have now passed 23 days of the 30-day window. Extensive English and local Macedonian searches reveal no reports of organized political violence, armed clashes, deadly riots, or terrorist incidents in February 2026; the only murders found were purely domestic/criminal, which ACLED does not record as fatalities. This lowers the probability considerably. Leaving room for the remaining 7 days of the window (~2%) and a margin for unreported events or data lags, I estimate a 5% chance of resolution.
